Context: In 2020, top executives at the bank resigned over a spying scandal targeting its former wealth management head, and last year, a court found that the bank failed to prevent money laundering by a Bulgarian cocaine trafficking gang The 167-year-old Credit Suisse already received a $50 billion (54 million Swiss francs) loan from the Swiss National Bank, which briefly caused a rally in the bank's stock price Last week, Credit Suisse’s biggest shareholder, Saudi National Bank, said that it would not be increasing funding to the bank for legal reasons after customers withdrew more than $133 billion last year

What directly CAUSED this event? A cause must happen BEFORE the event.

Event: Billions were withdrawn from Credit Suisse by investors and depositors.

Options:
A. Over $133 billion was withdrawn by Credit Suisse customers in 2022.
B. The Saudi National Bank declared it would not provide additional funding to Credit Suisse.
C. Credit Suisse faced substantial financial setbacks and controversies, including the resignation of senior executives in 2020 due to a spying scandal, a court ruling against them for inadequate money laundering controls, and a financial loss exceeding 7 billion Swiss francs in 2022.
D. Credit Suisse's stock hit an all-time low following the announcement by Saudi National Bank that it would not increase its financial support.

Answer with letter(s) only, comma-separated if multiple:
B,C,D

Context: In perhaps the most commonly cited recent paper on the matter, a group of economists led by MIT's David Autor found that between 1990 and 2007, Chinese import competition accounted for the loss of 1 million manufacturing jobs — about 25 percent of the total manufacturing job loss over that period Trump is Signing a Landmark Phase One Trade Agreement with China”, The Donald Trump administration — The White House archives, January 15, 2020, https://www (In fact, Trump may want to thank Xi for all his country's exports; those same economists plus Lund University's Kaveh Majlesi found in a January paper that, all other things being equal, if "the growth in Chinese import penetration had been 50 percent lower than the actual growth" in Wisconsin, Michigan and Pennsylvania than it was between 2000 and 2016, those states would have chosen Hillary Clinton, and she would have won the Electoral College
